summary: TypeScript compilation step failed: the UI TypeScript compile/lint hook (ts-compile-lint-ui) ran tsc and reported three TypeScript errors which caused the job to exit non-zero.
root_cause: The TypeScript compiler (tsc) reported type-checking / unused-declaration errors in the UI code which caused the ts-compile-lint-ui hook to fail: 'ColorMode' declared but never used, an unused imported type 'ParamsSpec', and a typing mismatch for SetupServer/SetupServerApi.

evidence.reason: Shows the failing lint/compile UI step.
Identifies the specific pre-commit hook that failed (TypeScript compile/lint for UI).

evidence.quote: src/layouts/Nav/UserSettingsButton.tsx:55:6 - error TS6196: 'ColorMode' is declared but never used.
evidence.reason: Exact TypeScript error indicating an unused type declaration in UserSettingsButton.tsx.

evidence.quote: src/pages/Connections/ConnectionForm.tsx:33:1 - error TS6133: 'ParamsSpec' is declared but its value is never read.
evidence.reason: Exact TypeScript error showing an unused imported type in ConnectionForm.tsx.

evidence.quote: testsSetup.ts:78:3 - error TS2739: Type 'SetupServer' is missing the following properties from type 'SetupServerApi': #private, network
evidence.reason: Exact TypeScript error showing a type incompatibility in testsSetup.ts.
Confirms the tsc run produced errors (not just warnings) and failed the build.

evidence.quote: subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['pnpm', 'tsc', '--p', '/home/runner/work/airflow/airflow/airflow-core/src/airflow/ui/tmpkhdkxx97.json']' returned non-zero exit status 2.
evidence.reason: Shows the tsc command returned a non-zero exit code, causing the CI step to fail.

suggested_fix: Run the TypeScript compiler locally (pnpm tsc --p src/airflow/ui/<tsconfig> or via the same tmp project) and fix the reported errors: remove or use the unused type declarations (e.g. 'ColorMode' and the 'ParamsSpec' import) or mark them appropriately, and update the test setup typing so the type of setupServer conforms to SetupServerApi (or adjust the types/casts). After fixing, re-run the ts-compile-lint-ui step (prek --all-files) or 'pnpm tsc' to confirm no errors remain.

summary: Logs show many DNS resolution failures and runner/agent activity but do not contain any explicit test failures or error messages from the Go test/run step, so the precise CI failure reason is not determinable from the provided logs alone.

evidence.quote: unable to resolve domain: cluster-api.example.com. err: unable to resolve domain cluster-api.example.com., status 0
evidence.reason: Shows repeated DNS resolution failures for a domain referenced in the logs, indicating network/DNS issues occurred during the run.
Indicates a service-runner status but does not show subsequent test failure messages or errors from the test runner.
Final log line shows cleanup activity but does not include explicit test results or failure traces to identify the root cause.

suggested_fix: Collect and provide the full CI job console output (including the go test/gotestsum output) and any failing test traces; meanwhile, investigate DNS/network resolution for domains referenced (e.g. cluster-api.example.com) by reproducing the job and checking resolver configuration or retrying the job.

summary: The Windows CI run failed because test.test_tcl had two assertion failures: Tcl results with surrogate characters were decoded incorrectly, producing mojibake instead of the expected surrogate escape representation.
root_cause: Two tests in test.test_tcl failed because the Tcl evaluation returned a string that was decoded as mojibake ('<ðŸ’»>') instead of the expected surrogate-escaped form ('<\ud83d\udcbb>' / '<\U0001f4bb>'), indicating an encoding/decoding mismatch when converting Tcl results to Python strings.

evidence.reason: Shows the failing test name and that it failed.
Shows the test expected the surrogate/Unicode escape representation '<\U0001f4bb>'.
Shows the actual returned string was '<ðŸ’»>' (mojibake) rather than the expected surrogate escape form.
Shows the second failing test in the same area (tcl evalfile surrogate handling).
Indicates the system locale encoding is cp1252, supporting the hypothesis that bytes were decoded using the ANSI/codepage encoding producing mojibake instead of UTF-8 decoding.
Shows the Tcl version in use (relevant to Tcl's handling/encoding of returned strings).

suggested_fix: Investigate and fix the encoding used when converting Tcl returned byte sequences to Python strings. Ensure Tcl results containing Unicode (e.g. emoji) are decoded as UTF-8 (or use surrogate-escape behavior expected by the tests). Concretely: adjust the Tcl<->Python bridge to decode Tcl result bytes with UTF-8 (or explicitly return surrogate escapes), or run the tests with a UTF-8 locale (so locale.getencoding is 'utf-8') so the decoding matches the test expectations.

metadata.provider_error: RuntimeError: diagnosis command exited 1: diagnosis_shim_openai: JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 28 column 16 (char 1414)

summary: The test run crashed with a native segmentation fault during numpy.argsort called from a datetime test (test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability).
root_cause: A segmentation fault occurred inside numpy.argsort (via numpy._core.fromnumeric._wrapfunc -> argsort) while running numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py::test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability, causing the pytest job to abort.

evidence.reason: Shows the run terminated due to a native segmentation fault.
Stack frame indicates the crash happened while executing numpy._core.fromnumeric._wrapfunc.
Stack frame shows numpy.argsort was on the call stack at the time of the crash.
Identifies the specific test that triggered the call to argsort which crashed.
CI runner exit code confirms abnormal termination due to the segmentation fault.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the failure locally by running the single test: pytest -q numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py::test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability. Run under a native debugger or sanitizer (e.g. build with debug symbols and ASAN or run under gdb/valgrind) to get a C-level backtrace. Investigate the implementation of argsort / the C extension code invoked by _wrapfunc for handling datetime/NaT values and recent changes to that code; if the crash only occurs with the built extensions in this CI environment, verify the extension build flags and rebuild with sanitizers enabled to pinpoint the invalid memory access.

summary: An assembly test failed because FileCheck did not find expected CHECK patterns in the generated assembly for wasm_exceptions.
root_cause: The assembly test /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs failed because FileCheck reported "expected string not found in input" when scanning the generated assembly file /checkout/obj/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions/wasm_exceptions.s, causing the test to fail.

evidence.reason: Shows which test failed in the suite.

evidence.quote: /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs:38:12: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input
evidence.reason: FileCheck reported an expected pattern at line 38 of the test file was not found in the assembly output.

evidence.quote: /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs:62:12: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input
evidence.reason: FileCheck reported another expected pattern at line 62 of the test file was not found.
Identifies the generated assembly file that was checked and did not contain the expected patterns.
Indicates that FileCheck verification (the test assertion) failed, causing the test to fail.
Shows part of the assembly around where FileCheck scanned; useful to compare against expected CHECK patterns mentioned in the test.

suggested_fix: Open /checkout/obj/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions/wasm_exceptions.s, compare it to the CHECK lines in /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs, and either update the CHECK expectations to match the actual assembly or adjust the codegen that emits the assembly so it produces the expected patterns. Re-run the assembly test locally (FileCheck) to verify the fix.
